entities.name: Master Peter of Ardene
entities.raw_quote: a certain surgeon from my home country, and in fact even a native of my very parish, named Master Peter of Ardene

entities.name: Spanish magician
entities.raw_quote: a certain Spanish magician [magus] was on close terms with him

entities.name: Demon (unnamed)
entities.raw_quote: the demon arrived, mourning and sighing, and with deep and plaintive sighs like a boy that has been beaten he said to them

entities.name: Virgin Mary (feast of the Assumption)
entities.raw_quote: the angels in heaven are celebrating the feast of the Virgin Mary… their punishment is augmented

entities.name: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifin, Nartim, Lodoni

attributes.role: named demonic names used in invocations

entities.raw_quote: by these names, which cause you fear and terror: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifin, Nartim and Lodoni

entities.name: Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor, Ym
entities.raw_quote: O Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor and Ym, arms-bearing spirits

entities.name: Lylet
entities.raw_quote: the conjuration mentioned above refers first to ‘all you demons inscribed on this ring’ and then addresses them twice by name… addresses the demon Lylet along with her companions (socii), who remain unnamed

entities.name: Aa, Ely, Sother, Adonay, Cel, Sabaoth, Messyas, Alazabra, Osian

attributes.role: sacred names of the Creator that bind demons

entities.raw_quote: these most precious and ineffable names of the Creator of all, by which you all are bound… Aa, Ely, Sother, Adonay, Cel, Sabaoth, Messyas, Alazabra and Osian

entities.name: Bismille araathe
entities.raw_quote: One notable exception is a conjuration given in pseudo-Chaldean (no. 18): Bismille araathe…. This formula actually derives from an Arabic protoype: Bismillahi ar-Rahma-n ar-Rahi-m

entities.name: Conjuration formulas (generic)
entities.raw_quote: the four primary elements virtually always found in conjurations… declaration (‘I conjure you’), the address (‘thus-and-such spirits’), the series of invocations, and the instruction

entities.name: Circle

attributes.function: containment and representation of spirit’s habitation

entities.raw_quote: by your dwelling place, of which this [circle] is the form

entities.name: Ring (magic)

attributes.function: inscribed with demonic names or spirits

entities.raw_quote: by this ring which is here… all you demons inscribed on this ring

entities.name: Child medium (puer/virgo iuvenis)
entities.raw_quote: the child conjure the spirit in the mother tongue… conjured to tell the truth… simply as puer or, more often, as ‘young virgin’ or ‘virginal youth’

summary: This chunk analyzes conjuration formulas in the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focusing on their structure (declaration, address, invocations, instruction) and content. It includes examples of named spirits (e.g., Rator et al., Vsyr et al., Lylet), sacred names used to bind demons (Aa, Ely, etc.), ritual tools (circle, ring), and a story exemplifying spirit-summoning involving Master Peter of Ardene and a Spanish magician. It also notes linguistic features (e.g., pseudo-Chaldean/Biblical/Arabic hybrid phrases) and comparative material from Jewish magic.
key_passages: The conjurations are so centrally important to necromantic experiments that the art of necromancy… can even be referred to simply as the conjuring of spirits.
Reduced to its generic essentials, the typical conjuration is in the form, ‘I conjure you, thus-and-such spirits, by the holy names, by all the saints of God, etc., to carry out my will.’
by these names, which cause you fear and terror: Rator, Lampoy, Despan, Brulo, Dronoth, Maloqui, Satola, Gelbid, Mascifin, Nartim and Lodoni

O Vsyr, Salaul, Silitor, Demor, Zanno, Syrtroy, Risbel, Cutroy, Lytay, Onor, Moloy, Pumotor, Tami, Oor and Ym, arms-bearing spirits, whose role it is to bear arms and to deceive human senses wherever you wish
by these most precious and ineffable names of the Creator of all… Aa, Ely, Sother, Adonay, Cel, Sabaoth, Messyas, Alazabra and Osian
